title: Fresh-slick option richness
summary: Replace a single fresh-slick feasibility flag with a richer description of how many slick choices can finish and which one is softest.
rationale: Duplicate-heavy public-submitted variants converged around this family, suggesting the signal is robust while many exact aliases are unnecessary. Counting viable options adds ordinal strategy information that a binary flag loses.
implementation_hint: For a hypothetical next-lap stop, compute per-compound finish margins for SOFT, MEDIUM, and HARD; add the number of viable fresh slicks, the softest viable slick ordinal, best and worst viable margins, and whether a slick different from the current compound can finish.
expected_effect: Medium ROC AUC gain with high confidence; expected to improve discrimination inside the final-stop window.
risk: Many near-duplicate aliases produced nearly identical predictions; implement one compact canonical version to avoid unnecessary feature bloat.
